sections.heading: 1. What is an Extension Function?
2. Why Use Extensions in Android?
sections.content:
In Android (XML-based), we often interact with the View class. Extensions help reduce repetitive code and improve readability.

Example: Simplifying View visibility handling.

sections.heading: 3. Extension Properties
sections.content: Extensions can also define properties. However, they cannot store state because they do not have backing fields. Only custom getters (or setters) are allowed.

sections.heading: 4. Resolving Conflicts
sections.content: Extension functions are resolved statically, meaning the function called is determined by the reference type at compile-time. If a class already has a member function with the same name and signature as an extension, the member function always takes priority.

sections.heading: 5. Useful Android Extensions (Real-world Examples)
sections.content: Common Android extensions that reduce boilerplate and improve readability.

sections.heading: Summary
items: Extensions allow adding logic to classes without modifying their source code.
Inside an extension, 'this' refers to the receiver object.
Extensions reduce boilerplate in Android (Toasts, View visibility, navigation).
They are resolved statically at compile-time.
Member functions always take priority over extensions with the same signature.
